summary: This brief post introduces a PDF infodeck co-authored with Pramod Sadalage that explains NoSQL databases to technical managers. The authors describe why relational databases dominated for so long and what forces disrupted that dominance. Rather than positioning NoSQL as a replacement, they emphasize that relational databases still have an important role, advocating for polyglot persistence—using different data storage technologies for different needs.
key_points: NoSQL databases represent an important shift in data storage that technical managers need to understand
Relational databases dominated for valid reasons, and specific forces led to NoSQL emerging as an alternative
The future isn't NoSQL replacing relational databases, but polyglot persistence—choosing the right storage technology for each use case
The content targets technical managers rather than developers, focusing on strategic understanding
money_quotes: We discuss the principal benefits of a NoSQL approach, but stress that relational databases still have a large role to play, thus our emphasis on polyglot persistence.
key_insight: The rise of NoSQL doesn't mean abandoning relational databases, but rather adopting polyglot persistence—strategically choosing different data storage technologies for different needs.
